source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
source.exclusions: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
source.population: People aged over 16 years with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
source.setting: Primary and specialist COPD diagnosis, monitoring, and ongoing management.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult COPD follow-up and exacerbation-feature documentation; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

items.rationale: Supports mapped spirometry, bronchodilator-response, respiratory-examination, and objective-test fields in suspected asthma.

items.final_wording: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.
